message:
chore(beads): close bd-mblr.1 coverage epic - all child tasks complete

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -796,7 +796,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-luay","title":"Thread bind parameters into JOIN/GROUP BY fallback expression evaluators","description":"execute_join_select and GROUP BY fallback evaluators currently ignore placeholders in eval_join_expr/evaluate_having_value paths, treating placeholders as NULL. Parameterized JOIN WHERE/ON and HAVING expressions can evaluate incorrectly. Add params-aware evaluation and tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-12T16:30:21.410228715Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T16:41:20.861576448Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T16:41:20.861557913Z","close_reason":"Parameterized JOIN/GROUP BY fallback placeholder binding is wired and validated via targeted tests: test_join_with_where_clause_anonymous_placeholder and test_group_by_join_having_named_placeholder.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-m0l2","title":"E2E: RaptorQ Integration Test Suite (Erasure Coding End-to-End)","description":"Unit Test Requirements:
- test_bd_m0l2_unit_compliance_gate: concrete unit identifier for structure lint compliance.
- prop_bd_m0l2_structure_compliance: concrete property identifier for structure lint compliance.

Cross-cutting E2E test suite for RaptorQ erasure coding integration across all subsystems.

COVERS: §3 (RaptorQ Foundation) + §3.4 (Integration Points) + §3.5 (ECS Substrate)

## TEST SCENARIOS

### Scenario 1: GF(256) Arithmetic
- test_e2e_gf256_all_field_operations: Verify add, mul, div, inv for all 256 elements
- test_e2e_gf256_generator_log_table: Verify generator=2 produces correct log/exp tables
- test_e2e_gf256_xor_patch_roundtrip: XOR delta encode/decode page patches, verify exact reconstruction

### Scenario 2: RaptorQ Encode/Decode
- test_e2e_raptorq_encode_decode_small: Encode 10 source symbols, lose 2, decode from any 10 of remaining, verify
- test_e2e_raptorq_encode_decode_large: Encode 10000 symbols (large database), verify decode with overhead < 2%
- test_e2e_raptorq_systematic_happy_path: Verify systematic symbols (ESI 0..K-1) are identity (no GF(256) needed)
- test_e2e_raptorq_failure_probability_monitoring: Verify failure probability tracking matches §3.1.1 bounds

### Scenario 3: Self-Healing WAL
- test_e2e_wal_fec_sidecar_created: After WAL write, .wal-fec sidecar exists with correct repair symbols
- test_e2e_wal_fec_repair_single_frame: Corrupt one WAL frame, repair from .wal-fec, verify identical to original
- test_e2e_wal_fec_repair_multiple_frames: Corrupt 3 of 10 frames, repair all, verify database integrity

### Scenario 4: ECS Object Lifecycle
- test_e2e_ecs_object_create_retrieve: Create ECS object (commit capsule), store as symbols, retrieve and decode
- test_e2e_ecs_object_id_content_addressed: Verify ObjectId = Trunc128(BLAKE3(header || payload)) is deterministic
- test_e2e_ecs_symbol_record_envelope: Create SymbolRecord with auth tag, verify envelope format matches §3.5.2
- test_e2e_ecs_root_manifest_bootstrap: Write RootManifest, shutdown, reopen, verify bootstrap recovery

### Scenario 5: Replication (Fountain-Coded)
- test_e2e_replication_sender_receiver: One node sends symbols, another receives and reconstructs database
- test_e2e_snapshot_shipping: Ship complete snapshot as fountain-coded stream, verify reconstruction

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Log every encode/decode: source symbols count, repair symbols generated, ESIs used
- Log GF(256) operations at TRACE level: operands, result, operation type
- Log ECS object lifecycle: create, store, retrieve, verify with ObjectId
- Log repair attempts: symbols available, symbols needed, success/failure, decode proof

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] RaptorQ decode succeeds with K source symbols (systematic happy path)
- [ ] RaptorQ decode succeeds with K+delta received symbols (delta < 2% overhead)
- [ ] .wal-fec sidecar repairs corrupted WAL frames when sufficient symbols exist
- [ ] ECS objects are content-addressed and deterministically reproducible
- [ ] Replication via fountain-coded symbols produces byte-identical database","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:42:38.155288630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T16:37:25.172760212Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T16:37:25.172734384Z","close_reason":"24 E2E tests passing: GF(256) arithmetic (4), RaptorQ encode/decode (6), self-healing WAL (3), ECS object lifecycle (4), replication (2), harness infra (2), compliance gates (3)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m0l2","depends_on_id":"bd-1fpm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T11:03:28.201818145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m0l2","depends_on_id":"bd-1hi.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:42:55.021268130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m0l2","depends_on_id":"bd-1hi.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:42:55.192720579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m0l2","depends_on_id":"bd-21c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:42:55.376147461Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":644,"issue_id":"bd-m0l2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Test Requirements (Normalization)

This bead is an E2E suite, but we still require unit tests for the RaptorQ harness pieces:

- test_symbol_loss_generator_deterministic (loss pattern derived from seed)
- test_decode_proof_verifier (DecodeProof validates without re-decoding)
- test_durability_estimator_monotonicity (p_upper changes update bound as expected)

## Logging Requirements (Normalization)

- INFO: K,R,T, loss_rate_est, p_upper, bound_P_loss
- On decode: symbols_used, decode_ms, proof_id
","created_at":"2026-02-08T10:11:16Z"},{"id":838,"issue_id":"bd-m0l2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Test Requirements
- test_bd_m0l2_unit_compliance_gate: verify section-specific unit validation hooks are wired.
- prop_bd_m0l2_structure_compliance: property check that required structure blocks are present.

## E2E Test
- test_e2e_bd_m0l2_compliance: run end-to-end scenario proving this bead's path with deterministic artifact capture.

## Logging Requirements
- DEBUG: emit stage-level progress and key branch decisions for this bead scope.
- INFO: emit summary counters and completion status for this bead scope.
- WARN: emit degraded-mode or retry conditions encountered during this bead scope.
- ERROR: emit terminal failure diagnostics with reproducible context for this bead scope.
- Reference: bd-1fpm structured repro-bundle logging conventions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit test identifiers for this bead are concrete and executable.
- [ ] E2E identifier for this bead is concrete and executable.
- [ ] Logging requirements include DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR and bd-1fpm alignment.","created_at":"2026-02-09T03:10:17Z"},{"id":906,"issue_id":"bd-m0l2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CloudyPond claiming focused compile/clippy-unblock slice: crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/bd_m0l2_raptorq_e2e_integration.rs (API alignment with current asupersync/ecs types).","created_at":"2026-02-09T16:06:35Z"},{"id":908,"issue_id":"bd-m0l2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CloudyPond completed API-alignment/compile-unblock slice in crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/bd_m0l2_raptorq_e2e_integration.rs: updated imports, switched systematic usage to emit_systematic/repair_symbol paths, adapted InactivationDecoder decode usage + ReceivedSymbol constructors, migrated OTI/SymbolRecord/ObjectId API changes, and refreshed small-roundtrip assumptions. Validation: cargo fmt -- <file>; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_cloudypond cargo check -p fsqlite-harness --test bd_m0l2_raptorq_e2e_integration; cargo clippy -p fsqlite-harness --test bd_m0l2_raptorq_e2e_integration -- -D warnings; cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --test bd_m0l2_raptorq_e2e_integration -- --nocapture (24/24 pass). Workspace snapshot now passes: cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo fmt --check.","created_at":"2026-02-09T16:12:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-mblr","title":"Coverage and E2E Logging Hardening Program","description":"Program-level epic to close quality gaps across measurement, testing realism, and diagnosability: (1) defensible coverage evidence, (2) comprehensive unit+integration+E2E verification with reduced mock-only confidence, and (3) consistently wired detailed structured logging for deterministic triage. Scope includes coverage instrumentation, unit-test expansion, no-mock real-backend testing, E2E scenario completeness, diagnostics schema standardization, and CI quality gates.","design":"Sequence: baseline -> gap map -> implementation tracks (unit, no-mock integration, E2E completeness, logging standardization) -> CI enforcement. All failures must be deterministic, reproducible, and artifact-backed.","acceptance_criteria":"Coverage baseline reproducible; realism inventory published; unit/integration/E2E critical paths each have non-mock evidence lanes where feasible; E2E scenario matrix complete and owned; structured logging schema wired and validated; CI gates enforce and fail on regressions with actionable artifacts.","notes":"Deferred intentionally for queue hygiene: this bead is a container/coordination umbrella, not a direct implementation lane. Work proceeds through its child tasks; reopen/close this bead during milestone rollup and completion review.","status":"deferred","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:23:07.642694571Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:51:03.694157141Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1337,"issue_id":"bd-mblr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Evidence snapshot for gap justification: README.md:133-141 states runtime still uses Phase 4 in-memory execution and snapshot persistence. crates/fsqlite-pager/src/traits.rs:341-447 exports MockMvccPager/MockTransaction/MockCheckpointPageWriter. crates/fsqlite-btree/src/traits.rs:143-172 exports MockBtreeCursor. crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/comparison.rs:294-303 defines ComparisonRunner::new_in_memory for both engines and many correctness tests call it (for example crates/fsqlite-e2e/tests/correctness_transactions.rs:28). crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/logging.rs:72-122 provides structured logging initialization, but crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/bin/e2e_runner.rs currently only parses verbose in config (lines 24, 118, 189) without wiring init_logging.","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:25:40Z"},{"id":1338,"issue_id":"bd-mblr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency intent: bd-mblr.1 produces objective baseline and risk map; bd-mblr.2 and bd-mblr.4 convert findings into real-backend tests and complete e2e scenarios; bd-mblr.5 standardizes and wires diagnostics so failures are explainable; bd-mblr.3 converts all requirements into CI-enforced gates. This ordering prevents premature gate hardening before measurement and observability are trustworthy.","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:25:43Z"},{"id":1350,"issue_id":"bd-mblr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fresh audit summary (2026-02-13): full no-mock unit coverage is not yet established, and e2e script logging is not yet uniformly schema-governed. Evidence snapshot: extensive mock/fake presence across unit paths (for example MockMvccPager/MockTransaction/MockBtreeCursor usage) and heterogeneous e2e script logging styles across e2e/*.sh. Added focused child tracks bd-mblr.4.5.* (script-suite completeness) and bd-mblr.5.5.* (script logging conformance) to close this gap with explicit acceptance criteria and CI enforcement dependencies.","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:29:38Z"},{"id":1351,"issue_id":"bd-mblr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan-space optimization pass (2026-02-13): tightened execution contracts for comprehensive unit coverage and full e2e script logging conformance without reducing scope. Added direct dependencies where artifact schema + deterministic seed contracts are required inputs, and strengthened acceptance criteria on drift gates so CI can enforce evidence quality (not just test count).","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:47:07Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-mblr.1","title":"Coverage Baseline and Gap Mapping","description":"Establish defensible coverage truth with tooling and a gap map. Output must quantify line/branch/function coverage and classify each test by realism level (mock, in-memory, file-backed, full-stack).","design":"Treat measurement artifacts as first-class outputs with deterministic commands and machine-readable exports.","acceptance_criteria":"Coverage metrics reproducible; test realism inventory complete; critical-path gap map published; policy thresholds approved.","notes":"Deferred intentionally for queue hygiene: this bead is a container/coordination umbrella, not a direct implementation lane. Work proceeds through its child tasks; reopen/close this bead during milestone rollup and completion review.","status":"deferred","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:23:16.794795914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:51:03.694559404Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.1","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:23:16.794795914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1339,"issue_id":"bd-mblr.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Detailed scope: (1) establish objective coverage baseline, (2) classify every test by realism level, (3) map gaps against critical invariants, (4) define enforceable thresholds. Key risk this epic addresses: coverage claims without instrumentation can create false confidence.","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:26:03Z"}]}

All 4 child tasks closed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.1","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:23:16.794795914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1339,"issue_id":"bd-mblr.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Detailed scope: (1) establish objective coverage baseline, (2) classify every test by realism level, (3) map gaps against critical invariants, (4) define enforceable thresholds. Key risk this epic addresses: coverage claims without instrumentation can create false confidence.","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:26:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-mblr.1.1","title":"Coverage Toolchain Baseline (line/branch/function)","description":"Add reproducible coverage commands and output artifacts for workspace and per-crate runs (line, branch, function). Include deterministic command wrappers, output paths, and a baseline report committed under version control.","design":"Use a single canonical command surface (wrapper/alias) that normalizes tooling flags, output paths, and profile settings to avoid drift between local and CI runs.","acceptance_criteria":"- Primary coverage command path selected and executable for workspace and representative per-crate slices.
- Artifacts include line/branch/function outputs in a stable directory layout keyed by commit/run metadata.
- Re-running on the same commit with the same flags yields reproducible summary numbers and file structure.","notes":"This is the root of the measurement chain: downstream realism, gap analysis, and CI gates should consume this contract rather than inventing ad-hoc coverage invocation logic.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:23:36.233554051Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:51:35.521880755Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T00:51:35.521861739Z","close_reason":"Completed: ADR-0001 for toolchain selection, scripts/coverage.sh for automation, baseline reports in docs/coverage/. Unblocks bd-mblr.1.2 and bd-mblr.1.3.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:23:36.233554051Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1344,"issue_id":"bd-mblr.1.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Deliverables: documented command set for workspace and per-crate coverage, committed sample reports, and CI-compatible invocation. Include branch coverage and function coverage, not only line coverage.","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:26:12Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-mblr.1.1.1","title":"Coverage toolchain ADR and selection","description":"Compare workspace-safe coverage options (llvm-cov/grcov/tarpaulin), pick one primary path, and record rationale/limitations. Output: decision record with exact commands and known caveats for this workspace.","design":"Evaluate tools using the same workspace slice and capture reproducibility + signal quality, not just installation convenience.","acceptance_criteria":"- ADR compares llvm-cov, grcov, and tarpaulin against workspace compatibility, branch/function fidelity, determinism, and CI runtime cost.
- ADR records exact chosen command(s), required tool versions, and fallback path if primary tool fails on a crate.
- ADR explicitly lists known blind spots so later metrics are interpreted correctly.","notes":"Decision quality here directly controls trustworthiness of every later SLO/gate discussion; avoid hand-wavy justification.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:37:50.503490213Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:49:04.481689135Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T00:49:04.481668767Z","close_reason":"Created ADR-0001 documenting cargo-llvm-cov as primary coverage tool with commands, rationale, and caveats. Added to decision register as dr-0007.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.1.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:37:50.503490213Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-mblr.1.1.2","title":"Automate reproducible coverage report generation","description":"Implement deterministic commands/scripts to emit line/branch/function coverage for workspace + per-crate slices. Output: reproducible artifact layout and invocation contract suitable for CI.","design":"Encapsulate all instrumentation flags and output routing in one entrypoint so local and CI execution are byte-for-byte aligned.","acceptance_criteria":"- Deterministic coverage command wrapper exists for workspace and per-crate runs.
- Wrapper emits both machine-readable summaries and human-readable reports with stable paths.
- CI can invoke the same wrapper without per-job custom flags.","notes":"Include run metadata (commit, rustc, profile, target set) in report headers for later forensic comparison.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:37:50.751262775Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:50:49.838887629Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T00:50:49.838868454Z","close_reason":"Created scripts/coverage.sh with workspace/crate/ci/report/breakdown commands. Supports threshold checking for CI gates, reproducible output artifacts in target/coverage/.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.1.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:37:50.751262775Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.1.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.1.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:38:30.328856191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
